title: Eyes on arts
bodyText:
GEORGE ENTERTAINMENT NEWS - Georgians can pick and choose their weekend entertainment from a large variety of events.

Below is our weekly calendar for the arts: Eyes on arts.These entries are sent to George Herald on a weekly basis. Have a look and book your tickets for some stunning entertainment on your doorstep.


> 19 June:


So Over the Rainbow (16:00) at the Market Community Theatre.

Solo performance by Rondo Mpiti-Spies, written by Mike van Graan and directed by Dion van Niekerk.

A multi-sketch satirical revue that delivers sharp, humorous commentary on global and local themes.

Tickets cost R120 at the door, or contact Heather on 072 369 3304.


> 20 June:


I Can Buy Myself Flowers (19:00) at the Market Community Theatre. Solo performance by Erika Breytenbach, written by Mike van Graan and directed by Toni Morkel. A comedy-drama centered on Natalie Jackson, a spirited estate agent in her 50s.

Through storytelling laced with humour and drama, Natalie reflects on relationships, parenting, ageing and societal expectations around gender and sex. Tickets cost R170 on Quicket, or contact Heather on 072 369 3304.


> 21 June:


To Life, With Love (19:00) at the Sedgefield Community Hall. Solo performance by John Maytham, written by Mike van Graan and directed by Josh Lindberg. This lyrical and poignant show explores the joys, challenges and profound beauty of existence.

With a script that weaves humour and tenderness, it is a heartfelt reflection on spousal, family and community bonds. Tickets cost R170 on Quicket, or contact Heather on 072 369 3304.


> 25 June-12 July:


The Sound of Music at the Arts Theatre. Evening performances and matinees, over a three-week period. A timeless tale of resilience, love and the transformative power of music, The Sound of Music follows the heartfelt journey of Maria, a spirited postulant from Nonnberg Abbey, set against the stunning backdrop of the Austrian Alps in 1938.

Sent to serve as governess for the seven children of the stern and widowed Captain Georg von Trapp, Maria brings joy, laughter and music back into their lives. As she wins the hearts of the children, and eventually the Captain, a tender romance blossoms.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
